compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
G3 container coverage review (parent 3fe1 + this MCP/llm-workflow sweep child): clean — no findings.

This child covers the MCP-surface + llm/workflow/ + llm-top-level slice of the parent and contributes to several parent criteria. 4-bucket map vs parent acceptance/success criteria for THIS pairing (absence cross-checked against the whole sibling roster):

- AC1 (logging substrate + stdout-purity test): structural — covered by sibling 8fbd.
- AC2 (exemplar llm/plan_review + enable BLE001/T201 + shrinking allowlist): structural — covered by 16a1.
- AC3 (~100 unguarded broad-except sites remediated in batches; no silent `except: pass` without justification): PARTIAL — this child remediates the mcp_server.py + llm/workflow/ + llm/ (top-level) packages to the observability floor. Remainder tiled by sibling sweeps 8d01 (__init__/reducer/graph/_store), d360 (grounding/_engine_support), fd72 (_cli/_commands), 0569 (_engine/rebar_reconciler). The AC3 package partition has no orphaned package.
- AC4 (narrowing surfaced a hidden bug → fix + regression test): PARTIAL — the mcp_server.py:892 masking-swallow fix (error-reporting path no longer hides the original failure) is a surfaced-bug instance for this pairing; pattern also set by 16a1 and exercised in 8d01/0569.
- AC5 (allowlist emptied; make lint + full suite pass; children parented w/ file-impact): PARTIAL — this child removes its own packages from the BLE001/T201 allowlist; final emptying + gate is 0ec1; this child is parented to 3fe1 with file_impact set.
- SC1 (one lint-enforced way): contributory — convention established by 16a1, finalized by 0ec1; this child conforms its surfaces (MCP raise/ToolError = the framework-native convention).
- SC2 (silent failures observable on stderr logger; KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit never swallowed; stdout pure incl. MCP framing): PARTIAL — this child uniquely covers the MCP-surface aspect: handlers raise (framework → isError) rather than catch-and-return error dicts, fixes the masking swallow, keeps the MCP path off stdout (JSON-RPC framing intact), and migrates llm/workflow + llm diagnostics to the named logger. The Base>-catch KI/SE landmines live in d360/0569; substrate in 8fbd.
- SC3 (future audit clean; new offenders fail CI): structural — gate enabled by 16a1, final audit by 0ec1.

Absence cross-check vs the whole sibling roster: no parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered, and no coverage gap is attributable to this pairing. The "keep the MCP server off stdout (no print)" scoping is consistent with the parent's stdout-is-sacred constraint (MCP-over-stdio reserves stdout for JSON-RPC framing). Clean — empty findings list.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
G4 child-consistency review (parent 3fe1 + this MCP-surface / llm-workflow / llm-top-level sweep child; whole sibling roster considered). CLEAN — empty findings list. All 7 cross-child interaction modes checked at the high-confidence / benign-reading bar; each is benign.

Verified against the codebase (read_file; note: the substring search tool returned spurious empty results on mcp_server.py and llm/, so all claims below are read_file-confirmed):
- mcp_server.py:892 carries exactly the nested `except Exception:` / `pass` in the `_bg` failure-reporting path (lines 887–893): the outer broad catch (887) is already justified `# noqa: BLE001 - reflected in run-state, not raised`; the INNER 892 swallow hides a failure of `run_finished(...)` while the code is reporting a failure — this is precisely the masking swallow the child names. The line cite is accurate (residual-reference mode clean).
- The MCP tool handlers already follow the raise convention: `transition_ticket`/`claim_ticket`/`reopen_ticket`/`edit_ticket`/etc. call `rebar.*`, which raise `RebarError`/`ConcurrencyError`; none catch-and-return error dicts (no `return {"error": ...}` exists in the file). So the child's "do NOT catch-and-return error dicts" is conformance-preserving, not a contradiction.

Mode-by-mode:

- Implicit shared state: clean. This child removes only its own packages (mcp_server.py, llm/workflow/, llm/ top-level) from the BLE001/T201 allowlist. The allowlist is authored by 16a1, each sweep removes its own slice, 0ec1 empties it — sequenced via blocks edges (16a1 → ceaa → 0ec1), so no concurrent writer at this child's time.

- Conflicting assumptions: clean. "Use ToolError for intentional client-facing messages" — `ToolError` is not yet imported anywhere (read_file confirms `ConcurrencyError` is the raised type, e.g. handler docstrings at mcp_server.py:713,721 already say "raises a tool error"). Introducing/using ToolError for clean client messages is consistent with the parent's stated framework-native convention (raised exception → isError result), and a benign forward-looking instruction, not a contradiction with any sibling. "Keep the server off stdout (no print)" is consistent with the parent's stdout-is-sacred constraint (MCP-over-stdio reserves stdout for JSON-RPC framing) and with substrate 8fbd's "install the MCP stderr handler at main, never stdout."

- Scope overlap: clean (benign). `src/rebar/mcp_server.py` is in both 8fbd's and this child's file_impact, but the edits are DISTINCT and non-conflicting: 8fbd installs the stderr StreamHandler at `main` (line 901–902 today, explicitly "no except changes"); this child does the raise/ToolError remediation + the 892 swallow fix. Correctly ordered (8fbd blocks 16a1; 16a1 blocks ceaa), so the substrate's handler lands before this sweep touches except bodies. 8fbd's own G4 review already classified this overlap as benign.

- Ordering violation: clean. This child consumes the logger substrate (8fbd) and the convention+allowlist enable (16a1); both are inbound prerequisites via 8fbd → 16a1 → ceaa, and ceaa → 0ec1 (close-out). Chain consistent — the named logger exists before llm/workflow diagnostics migrate to it.

- Dependency gap: clean for THIS pairing. The un-owned `src/rebar/_io/` package (7 pre-seeded `# noqa: BLE001` sites in import_ndjson.py) is a real roster hole, but it is OUTSIDE this child's stated mcp_server / llm/workflow / llm-top-level scope and is already filed against 16a1 (allowlist author) and 0ec1 (allowlist-empty gate). Per the absence cross-check I do not re-attribute it here.

- Consumer impact: clean. The 892 fix only changes the error-reporting path (it cannot hide the original failure); the surrounding LWW run-record contract (record 'running' before return; background overwrite, lines 864–896) is preserved. `run_workflow` still returns `{run_id, ticket_id, status:'running'}` as a tool result; no process-stdout/JSON-RPC framing is touched. No verified external consumer of an error-dict return exists to break (there are none). The llm/ top-level + llm/workflow diagnostic→logger migration surfaced no read_file-confirmed `file=sys.stderr` consumer test in those packages.

- Residual references: clean. The `mcp_server.py:892` cite is exact (verified); no dangling code/test reference is introduced.

No high-confidence cross-child contradiction, scope conflict, or ordering gap is attributable to this (parent + MCP/llm-workflow sweep child) pairing. Clean.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS 1 review (E4 — assumption/premise verification). Findings below; several load-bearing premises appear already-satisfied in the repo, which should be reconciled before this sweep is treated as net-new work.

AFFIRMATIONS (E4 criteria passed):
- The plan names concrete, verifiable locations (`src/rebar/mcp_server.py:892`, `llm/workflow/`, `llm/` top-level, the BLE001/T201 allowlist) rather than vague gestures — each assertion is probe-able, which is exactly what E4 wants.

FINDINGS:

- [ ] Verify the premise that `mcp_server.py:892` still has an unfixed masking swallow before scoping it as work. [criteria: E4 | location: src/rebar/mcp_server.py:890-904 / plan "What" + AC2] The plan asserts a nested `except Exception: pass` at line 892 "masks the real error WHILE reporting a failure." Read of lines 890-904 shows NO `pass`: the inner `except Exception:` (line 895) logs BOTH the recorder failure (with `exc_info=True`) and the original run error (`logger.warning("failed to record workflow run %s failure (original run error: %s)", run_id, exc, exc_info=True)`). `search_files "except Exception: pass"` in mcp_server.py returns zero hits. pyproject.toml:218 states "The mcp_server.py:892 masking swallow was fixed (the failure-recording path now logs instead of swallowing)." If the fix already landed, AC2 is already met — re-scope or close.

- [ ] Verify the premise that these packages are still on the BLE001/T201 allowlist. [criteria: E4 | location: pyproject.toml:202-228 / plan "What" bullet 4 + AC4] The plan calls to "Remove these packages from the BLE001/T201 allowlist." pyproject.toml has NO per-file-ignores entry for `src/rebar/mcp_server.py`, `src/rebar/llm/workflow/*`, or `src/rebar/llm/*` (top-level). Lines 217-220 explicitly read "MCP surface (mcp_server.py) + llm/workflow/ + llm top-level — SWEPT by ceaa; removed." The removal the plan proposes appears already done.

- [ ] Verify the premise that MCP handlers currently return structured error dicts and need `ToolError` introduced. [criteria: E4 | location: src/rebar/mcp_server.py / plan "What" bullet 1 + AC1] `search_files "ToolError"` and `"return {\"error\""` in mcp_server.py both return zero hits. `_logging.py:42` already documents the framework's `isError` raise-convention. The plan should cite the specific handler(s) still returning error dicts (an unverifiable "ensure handlers surface errors" without a current-offender citation is, per E4, fail-closed a gap) — or confirm the convention is already adopted.

Net: the repo state strongly suggests this sweep (epic ring-gun-jot ceaa) already landed. Please reconcile each AC against current `main`; if confirmed-done, this should be closed/superseded rather than re-planned.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS 1 review (G1G2 — edit-set/scope accuracy). Findings below. The named edit targets all EXIST (mcp_server.py, llm/workflow/, llm/ top-level, the pyproject allowlist), so no hallucinated targets. However, scope-accuracy checks surfaced that the described work appears to already be present in the tree — the plan should reconcile its premise against current state.

AFFIRMATIONS:
- Every file/symbol named exists: src/rebar/mcp_server.py, src/rebar/llm/workflow/ and src/rebar/llm/ (top-level) packages, and the [tool.ruff.lint.per-file-ignores] allowlist in pyproject.toml are all real. ToolError is a real, used convention (tests/interfaces, _logging.py:43).
- Blast radius is contained to the named dirs + pyproject; no unenumerated cross-dir consumer of a *behavioral* change was found (the allowlist edit is config-local; raising vs. returning error-dicts is internalized by the FastMCP framework, with the adapter at tests/interfaces/adapters.py already mapping ToolError→Outcome).

FINDINGS:

- [ ] Reconcile the plan against current tree state: the "What"/AC describe work that pyproject.toml lines 217-220 say is already DONE ("MCP surface ... SWEPT by ceaa; removed"). [G1G2]
  criteria: G1G2
  location: src/rebar/mcp_server.py:892; pyproject.toml:217-220; AC lines 1-4
  evidence: (1) The plan targets the masking swallow at mcp_server.py:892, but the live code at 882-904 already logs both errors with `logger.warning(..., exc_info=True)` under `except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — the failure-recording path must not mask the original run error` — there is no `except Exception: pass` (grep for "except Exception: pass" in mcp_server.py = 0 hits). (2) pyproject.toml:217 reads "MCP surface (mcp_server.py) + llm/workflow/ + llm top-level — SWEPT by ceaa; removed." — i.e. these packages are already OUT of the allowlist; the AC "These packages removed from the allowlist" would be a no-op. (3) No error-dict returns (grep `return {"error"` = 0) and no `print(` in mcp_server.py. The plan should either (a) confirm it documents work already landed and scope down to verification/AC-checkoff, or (b) if there is residual work, point to the specific surviving non-conformant sites, because the broad targets named read as already-satisfied.

- [ ] Pin the exact handler sites still returning structured error dicts (if any), rather than the package-level claim. [G1G2]
  criteria: G1G2
  location: src/rebar/mcp_server.py (AC line 1)
  evidence: ABSENCE — AC1 asserts handlers "raise ... rather than returning structured error dicts," but a grep for error-dict returns in mcp_server.py yields no hits, so the in-scope behavioral hunk is unverifiable from the plan text. Enumerate the specific offending handler(s)/line(s) so the change is classifiable as in-scope; otherwise this hunk reads as already-out-of-scope (nothing to change).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — rubric A1 (anti-slop / over-engineering / NIH / config-proliferation / DSO anti-pattern set): CLEAN, no findings.

Affirmations:
- No new abstraction introduced. Plan adopts framework-native `raise` + the `mcp` framework's own `ToolError` primitive (src/rebar/mcp_server.py:296 imports FastMCP from that framework). No swallow-helper or error-mapper module is proposed — consistent with the explicit codebase stance at src/rebar/_logging.py:33 ("There is intentionally no shared swallow helper... it would be novel").
- Not NIH. raise→isError + ToolError is the documented convention (_logging.py:41-43); the "observability floor" is an existing standard, not a rebuild.
- No config-surface proliferation: the plan REMOVES per-file-ignores allowlist entries (pyproject.toml:217-224) rather than adding config keys.
- No golden-hammer / cargo-cult / resume-driven / premature-optimization signals in scope.

Out-of-A1-scope note (not a finding for this rubric): pyproject.toml:217-220 describes this exact sweep as "SWEPT by ceaa" with mcp_server.py:892 already fixed and "No prints existed in this scope." A reviewer on a duplication/staleness rubric may want to confirm whether this plan re-does already-completed work; that concern is outside A1's abstraction/NIH/config remit.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — rubric **G6** (approach soundness / anti-patterns / alternative-selection). Mostly PASS; one mechanism-completeness coaching finding on an edge case the plan under-specifies. (I deliberately do NOT re-raise the "this sweep already landed" concern — that is E4/G1G2 territory and is well covered above; G6 judges the chosen approach on its own terms.)

AFFIRMATIONS (G6 criteria passed):
- MECHANISM CORRECTNESS: the chosen mechanism actually achieves the goal. MCP handlers `raise` (framework → `isError` result) + `ToolError` for client-facing messages is exactly the canonical convention documented at `src/rebar/_logging.py:41-43`; the 892 fix the plan describes (error-reporting path logs BOTH the recorder failure with `exc_info=True` AND the original run error rather than swallowing) is the correct shape for a daemon-thread failure path that has no caller to re-raise to (`mcp_server.py:882-904`). Data-flow is complete and the failure case is handled.
- FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE: solves the named problem (masking swallow + non-conformant broad-excepts + allowlist removal), not a proxy.
- APPROACH SELECTION (alternatives generated, then discarded as the chosen approach is defensible): (a) a centralized try/except decorator/wrapper around all MCP handlers mapping domain errors→ToolError — REJECTED as worse: `_logging.py:33` explicitly states "There is intentionally no shared swallow helper… it would be novel," so per-handler raise is the codebase-aligned, lower-blast-radius choice; (b) at 892, re-raise-the-original vs. log-both — log-both is correct because a background daemon thread has no caller, so re-raise would just terminate the thread silently. Chosen approach is at-least-as-good on alignment/blast-radius/simplicity/robustness. Alternatives discarded; no finding.
- POSITIVE RATIONALE present: the plan states WHY ("adopts the framework-native convention"; ToolError "for clean client-facing messages"; the 892 rationale "so the error-reporting path cannot hide the original failure"). Sub-criterion (4) satisfied.

FINDING:

- [ ] Specify the correct treatment for the two `except BaseException` cleanup-and-reraise sites in scope so the "observability floor" instruction is not mis-applied to them.
  - criteria: G6
  - location: src/rebar/llm/workflow/prompt_authoring.py:231 and src/rebar/llm/workflow/snapshot.py:208 (plan "What" bullet 3 + AC3)
  - evidence: The plan's "Bring broad-excepts … to the observability floor; migrate stderr diagnostics to the named logger" is mechanism-INCOMPLETE for a failure-case edge it does not name. Two in-scope sites are `except BaseException:` blocks that intentionally catch KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit to run cleanup and then **re-raise** — the atomic-write temp-file unlink (`prompt_authoring.py:227-236`) and the subprocess child-reap (`snapshot.py:208-218`). The correct floor treatment here is "catch-all → cleanup → `raise`, with a justified `# noqa: BLE001`" (per `_logging.py:35-39`, which permits a justified noqa and warns KI/SE "are never swallowed"), NOT "log-and-continue" and NOT "narrow to `Exception`." An implementer reading the generic instruction could (wrongly) narrow these to `Exception` — which would let KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit skip the temp-file unlink / child-reap and leak a temp file or a zombie child — or convert the re-raise into a log-and-swallow. The as-built code already carries the right justified noqas (pyproject.toml:218-220 documents "the two cleanup-and-reraise `except BaseException` sites (prompt_authoring/snapshot) carry justified noqas"), but the PLAN TEXT does not surface this special case, so the mechanism is under-specified for an implementer who only has the plan.
  - suggested_fix: Add a sentence to "What" bullet 3: "Note: the two `except BaseException` cleanup-and-reraise sites (prompt_authoring.py atomic-write temp-file unlink; snapshot.py child-reap) must stay broad and re-raise (so KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit still run cleanup) — meet the floor via a justified `# noqa: BLE001` reason string, NOT by narrowing or by log-and-continue."

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS-1 review (G6 — approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection):

AFFIRMATIONS:
- MECHANISM CORRECTNESS: the proposed mechanism is sound and complete. Handlers `raise` (framework converts to an `isError` result) with `ToolError` reserved for intentional client-facing messages — the FastMCP-native convention. Verified against `src/rebar/mcp_server.py`: a named `logger` already exists (line 23) for the diagnostic-migration target, and the :892 fix the plan names is correctly specified — the failure-recording path logs BOTH the recorder error (with `exc_info=True`) and the original run error `exc`, so the error-reporting path can no longer mask the original failure (lines 890-904).
- FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE: the sweep solves the named problem (broad-excepts swallowing failures, stdout pollution on the MCP-over-stdio path) rather than a proxy. Keeping the server off stdout is correctly load-bearing for JSON-RPC framing (see `main()` / `install_stderr_handler` at lines 912-918).
- APPROACH SELECTION: I generated a structurally-different alternative — a centralized error-handling decorator/wrapper around every tool handler instead of per-handler `raise`/`ToolError`. The plan's choice is defensibly at-least-as-good: it leans on the framework's native `isError` conversion (lower blast radius, no new abstraction, codebase-aligned with the existing `# noqa` convention). No clearly-superior alternative was missed; discarding mine. The background-thread broad-except (line 890) is correctly preserved as data-in-state rather than raised — a justified exception to the raise convention, not an anti-pattern.

FINDINGS:
- [ ] State a POSITIVE rationale for choosing `raise`/`ToolError` over returning structured error dicts. The plan asserts the convention ("the framework converts to an `isError` result") but does not say WHY this is the right fit — e.g. that returning error dicts as normal results makes failures indistinguishable from success at the protocol layer, defeating client-side error handling, whereas `raise` lets FastMCP set `isError` and `ToolError` carries clean client-facing text. criteria: [G6]; location: "What" section / AC line 1 ("MCP tool handlers raise..."); evidence: the plan names the mechanism but the only rationale is the mechanical "framework converts" — the fitness argument (protocol-level distinguishability of error vs. success) is absent.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: MCP-surface sweep landed (commit c212b4b5). Plan-review PASS (0 blocking, 1 advisory), signed serially at the commit (HEAD stable). mcp_server.py:892 masking swallow FIXED — failure-recording path now logs both the recorder error (exc_info) and the original run error, masking neither. The 2 except BaseException sites (prompt_authoring atomic-write, snapshot child-reap) verified to clean-up-AND-reraise (interrupts propagate) — kept BaseException + justified noqa, NOT narrowed (distinct from d360's harness which swallowed). Step/yaml/lint/reduce/tool-boundary/parity/completion fallbacks justified-noqa (in-band capture or fail-open). No prints in scope. Removed mcp_server + llm/workflow + 4 llm top-level files from the allowlist. Code-review subagent: clean (confirmed BaseException re-raise). Full suite run ALONE (serialized): 3210 passed/12 skipped, HEAD stable — confirms the earlier pollution was concurrent-subagent worktree isolation, not a code defect. make lint green.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
**Completion verification: PASS** — all 4 acceptance criteria met.

**AC1 (handlers raise, no print to stdout):** `search_files` confirms zero `return {"error"` hits and zero `print(` hits in `mcp_server.py`. All tool handlers let domain exceptions propagate for framework→isError conversion.

**AC2 (mcp_server.py:892 masking swallow fixed):** `read_file(mcp_server.py:890–904)` shows the inner `except Exception` (line 895) carries `# noqa: BLE001 — the failure-recording path must not mask the original run error` and logs both the recorder error (`exc_info=True`) and the original run error `exc` via `logger.warning(...)`. No `pass` swallow. Fix confirmed.

**AC3 (broad-excepts in llm/workflow/ and llm/ top-level meet observability floor):** All broad-excepts in `src/rebar/llm/workflow/` carry justified `# noqa: BLE001` reason strings. The two `except BaseException:` cleanup sites (`prompt_authoring.py:231`, `snapshot.py:208`) clean up and then `raise` — interrupts propagate, never swallowed. Top-level llm files (`completion.py`, `parity.py`, `pai_tools.py`, `config.py`, `tracing.py`, `prompts.py`) all have justified noqas on every broad-except.

**AC4 (packages removed from allowlist):** `pyproject.toml:202–228` has no per-file-ignores entries for `src/rebar/mcp_server.py`, `src/rebar/llm/workflow/*`, or `src/rebar/llm/*`. Comment at line 217 records "MCP surface (mcp_server.py) + llm/workflow/ + llm top-level — SWEPT by ceaa; removed." Suite result from final commit comment: 3210 passed / 12 skipped, make lint green.

compiled_state.description:
# Sweep: MCP surface (mcp_server.py) + llm/workflow/ + llm top-level — raise/ToolError, fix masking swallow

The MCP surface adopts the framework-native convention: tool handlers `raise` (the framework converts to
an `isError` result); use `ToolError` for intentional client-facing messages. Includes a specific fix:
the nested `except Exception: pass` at `mcp_server.py:892` masks the real error WHILE reporting a failure.

## What
- `src/rebar/mcp_server.py`: ensure tool handlers surface errors by raising (map domain errors like
  `ConcurrencyError`/validation to `ToolError` for clean client messages); do NOT catch-and-return error
  dicts as normal results. Keep the server off stdout (no `print`).
- Fix the masking swallow at `src/rebar/mcp_server.py:892` so the error-reporting path cannot hide the
  original failure.
- Bring broad-excepts in `src/rebar/llm/workflow/` and `src/rebar/llm/` (top-level) to the observability
  floor; migrate stderr diagnostics to the named logger.
- Remove these packages from the BLE001/T201 allowlist as they become conformant.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] MCP tool handlers raise (or raise `ToolError`) rather than returning structured error dicts; no
      `print` reaches stdout from the MCP path.
- [ ] `mcp_server.py:892` no longer masks the original error.
- [ ] Broad-excepts in `llm/workflow/` and `llm/` (top-level) meet the observability floor.
- [ ] These packages removed from the allowlist; `make lint` + full test suite pass.

file_impact.reason: fix masking swallow at :892 (failure-recording path now logs both errors); module logger
2 BaseException cleanup-reraise noqas; step/yaml/lint/reduce fallbacks noqa
remove mcp_server, llm/workflow, llm top-level from allowlist

compiled_state.title: Sweep: MCP surface (mcp_server) + llm/workflow + llm top-level — raise/ToolError, fix masking swallow
